from core.adjustments.hdr_toning import apply_hdr_toning, HDRToningDialog

print\('apply_hdr_toning OK, format:', out.format\(\)\)

Bash(cd "/home/lucial/Git Projects/linuxphotoshop" && python3 -c "
from PyQt6.QtGui import QImage, QColor
from core.adjustments.hdr_toning import apply_hdr_toning, HDRToningDialog

img = QImage\(40, 30, QImage.Format.Format_ARGB32_Premultiplied\)
img.fill\(QColor\(120, 80, 200\)\)

out = apply_hdr_toning\(img, radius=5, strength=0.6, gamma=1.2, detail=0.3\)
print\('apply OK, format:', out.format\(\)\)

# Check caching: simulate dialog usage
import numpy as np
from ui.adjustments_dialog import _bits_ba, _to_argb32
src = _to_argb32\(img\)
ba, arr = _bits_ba\(src\)
orig_f = arr[:,:,:3].astype\(np.float32\)/255.0

from core.adjustments.hdr_toning import _gauss_blur, _hdr_combine
blurred = _gauss_blur\(orig_f, 5\)
print\('blur shape:', blurred.shape, 'dtype:', blurred.dtype\)
res = _hdr_combine\(np, orig_f, blurred, 0.5, 1.0, 0.2\)
print\('combine shape:', res.shape, 'dtype:', res.dtype\)
print\('All OK'\)
")
